nodes.name: 艾伯塔省
nodes.summary: 艾伯塔省是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联9种鸟类，包括Black-billed cuckoo、Mountain plover、星蜂鸟等9种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: Curve-billed thrasher
nodes.summary: Curve-billed thrasher（Curve-billed thrasher，学名 Toxostoma curvirostre），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联9个分布地点节点。

nodes.name: 美國西南部
nodes.summary: 美國西南部是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联17种鸟类，包括Least grebe、黑頸鸊鷉、走鵑等17种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The curve-billed thrasher (Toxostoma curvirostre) is a medium-sized mimid native to most of Mexico and to the deserts of southwestern United States

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The curve-billed thrasher (Toxostoma curvirostre) is a medium-sized mimid native to most of Mexico and to the deserts of southwestern United States

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The curve-billed thrasher (Toxostoma curvirostre) is a medium-sized mimid native to most of Mexico and to the deserts of southwestern United States

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: thumb|300px|On a saguaro, whose nearly open blooms will provide important food|alt=Bird on a cactus The curve-billed thrasher is commonly found throughout the southwestern United States from Arizona's Sonoran Desert across New Mexico to west Texas, southeastern Colorado and southwestern Kansas as well as most of Mexico from the Sonoran-Chihuahuan Deserts and south through the Mexican Plateau into Central Tamaulipas, inland to Oaxaca, and on the coast of Sonora to Nayarit. Vagrants have been found in bordering states of its range, as far north as North Dakota, Alberta and Manitoba and as far east as Florida. Palmeri types have been spotted in Florida, while curvirostre have been found in Iowa on several occasions. Other than previously noted exceptions, the curve-billed is essentially non-vagrant. In comparison with other desert thrashers, the curve-billed is not as particular with habitats and can be found from sea-level up to . It generally resides where cholla and saguaro cacti, ocotillo, mesquites, palo verde, and creosote bushes are prevalent. It can also be located along woodland edges, piñon, dry desert bushland and areas within its region where cacti are present. If there is an adequate amount of desert vegetation near human habitation, along with feeders, the curve-billed can adapt within these environments
